---
description: "Use when editing the Streamlit app, Streamlit support helpers, or their tests. Covers session isolation, background crawl jobs, progress/cancel events, downloads, and container startup."
applyTo: "apps/streamlit/**"
---

# Streamlit App

Browser UI for users who prefer a form-based crawl workflow instead of the notebook.

## Related Skills

- Before Streamlit app work, read `.agents/skills/developing-with-streamlit/SKILL.md`.
- Load the relevant Streamlit sub-skill from `.agents/skills/` for the specific task, such as layout, session state, performance, CLI, markdown, or design work.

## Constraints

- `apps/streamlit/streamlit_app.py` owns the shared app shell and global Streamlit session state. Keep crawl/job helpers in `apps/streamlit/src/crawl4md_streamlit/support.py`.
- Page modules live under `apps/streamlit/app_pages/`. They must expose `render_page()` and render content-area UI only.
- When a page needs shell-owned callbacks or shared runtime state, pass a small context object from `streamlit_app.py` into the page module instead of importing `streamlit_app.py` from the page module.
- Prefix page-specific session keys with the page id when adding complex state to Steps 2-5, for example `vector_index_*` or `rag_qa_*`.
- `crawl4md_streamlit.support` must not import Streamlit. Keep it pure Python so it stays unit-testable.
- Deployment-tunable, non-secret config lives in `crawl4md_streamlit.settings` (`pydantic-settings` `Settings` + cached `get_settings()`), loaded from `.env.defaults` (committed) then `.env` (git-ignored) then the process environment. `Settings` fields are **required** with no in-code fallbacks, so `.env.defaults` is the single source of truth for every default (a missing key fails fast). The form/UI default constants (`form_defaults`, `vector_form_ui`, `llm_form_ui`), the app download/preview limits, the semantic-search top-N, and the session-retention window read from it; keep the libraries pure (no env reads beyond cloud auth). Secrets (`AWS_*`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) stay plain environment variables — `.env` locally, root-level keys in `.streamlit/secrets.toml` on Streamlit Community Cloud — never in `settings`.
- **Anti-bot escalation (Step 1)** is deployment-configured, not per-crawl form input: `build_configs` reads `proxies` from the `CRAWL_PROXIES` secret env and `undetected_browser` from `settings.crawl_undetected_browser` (`CRAWL_UNDETECTED_BROWSER`); `start_crawl_job` builds an optional scraping-API fallback from `CRAWL_FALLBACK_API_URL`/`CRAWL_FALLBACK_API_TOKEN` and passes it to `SiteCrawler(fallback_fetch_function=...)`. Proxies and the fallback API are secrets — env / `.streamlit/secrets.toml` only, never `.env.defaults` or logs.
- The multipage shell uses native `st.navigation` in `streamlit_app.py`. Keep `crawl4md_streamlit.pages` pure; it owns page metadata only and must not import Streamlit. `st.navigation` is registered on every run *before* the session-bootstrap `st.stop()` gate so deep-linked page URLs (e.g. `/vector-index`) survive hydration reruns; do not move it back below the gate.
- The shared page shell renders the title/subtitle, session controls, language selector, page content, footer, and portfolio modal. Do not duplicate any of these inside page modules.
- App-wide notifications, including progress toasts that should appear on every workflow page, belong in the shared shell rather than individual page modules. Do not call `st.toast()` in `apps/streamlit/app_pages/**`; if a future page needs an app-wide toast, pass a shell-owned callback/context. Keep page-local feedback inline with page content (`st.info`, `st.warning`, `st.success`, or page panels).
- **Steps 3-5 — Semantic Search / RAG Q&A / Conversational RAG** are implemented and inherit the shared page shell. Each page receives a `RagPageContext` from the shell (`default_language`, `list_indexes`, `render_downloads`) and renders an explicit **index picker** built from `index_catalog.list_session_indexes` (pure: discovers successful indexes under the session root). All RAG logic lives in the UI-independent `rag_engine` library (`retrieve`, `answer_question`, `chat_answer`); pages are thin and only collect input, call it, and render the structured `RagAnswer`/`RetrievalResult`. RAG runs **synchronously** (a spinner), not as a background job.
  - **Step 3 (Semantic Search)** selects an index first (wrapped in a card), shows its `manifest.json` metadata inside that card in a collapsible **Details** expander (collapsed by default) as a compact **4-column grid** via `render_index_metadata` (built from the pure `index_metadata_rows` helper, including the manifest `created_at` formatted as UTC), runs a search (the query + Search button live in a form, while a **Search options** expander sits *above* the form so its widgets stay reactive — exposing search mode similarity/MMR with **Diversity** + **Candidate pool** grouped on the same row and disabled unless MMR is selected via the pure `mmr_controls_enabled` helper, plus minimum-similarity and a source-file filter built from `manifest.indexed_sources`; a top-N number input sits beside the query, defaulting to `settings.semantic_search_top_n` — all flowing into `RagConfig`), and renders ranked result cards via `render_ranked_results` (one bordered card per hit: title with the chunk id/size/language caption beneath it + right-docked similarity **text**, chunk text in **Raw/Preview** tabs — Raw first by default, configurable via `SEMANTIC_SEARCH_DEFAULT_TAB`, each tab's text wrapped in its own card so the tabs read as UI — from the pure `result_detail_caption`/`ordered_result_tabs` helpers, sorted score-descending). It ends with the shared Output Files section (`context.render_downloads()`), mirroring Step 2. Leave `render_sources` (used by Steps 4-5) unchanged. The pure card helpers (including `mmr_controls_enabled`) live in `rag_ui` and are unit-tested in `test_rag_ui.py`.
  - Steps 4-5 add the chat-model selector + retrieval-depth control via `llm_form_ui` (pure, testable `chat_model_options`/`chat_model_label` helpers) and share render helpers from `rag_ui` (`select_index`, `render_sources`, `render_messages`, `render_model_caption`, `to_chat_turns`). Step 5 is a chat UI (`st.chat_message`/`st.chat_input`) with history in `st.session_state` and history-aware query rewriting from `rag_engine`.
  - Prefix Steps 3-5 session keys with `semantic_search_` / `rag_qa_` / `conversational_rag_`. Localize `rag_engine` warnings/errors with `i18n.localize_message` (codes `rag.*`); never substring-match library text. New RAG UI strings live in `i18n/en.py` + `i18n/id.py` (and the `Strings` TypedDict).
- **Step 2 — Build Vector Index** is implemented and mirrors Step 1's shell. The page (`app_pages/vector_index.py`) receives a `VectorIndexPageContext` from the shell and only collects form input and calls callbacks. Indexing logic lives in the UI-independent `vector_indexer` library; the background job lives in `vector_index_jobs.py` (mirrors `crawl_jobs.py` — thread + event queue + cooperative cancel). Do not put indexing business logic in the app.
  - Prefix Step 2 session keys with `vector_index_` and keep them separate from crawl keys. Reuse the shared `dialog_ui.render_confirm_dialog` helper with its own handler, `vector_stop_*` button keys, and **vector-specific wording** (`VEC_DIALOG_STOP_BODY`/`VEC_DIALOG_BTN_STOP` — "Stop indexing", never the crawl strings); do not modify `crawl_jobs.py` or the crawl stop handler.
  - Reuse the existing output-files display (`_render_downloads`) and the form action buttons (`crawl_action_buttons`); the form lives in `vector_form_ui.py` with pure, testable option/validation helpers (`crawl_result_options`, `has_index_inputs`, `resolve_embedding_model_choices`). The embedding-model dropdown order and pre-selected default are operator-tunable via `VECTOR_EMBEDDING_MODELS`/`VECTOR_DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL` (validated against the library's `EMBEDDING_MODEL_OPTIONS`; default `all-MiniLM-L6-v2`, listed first).
  - Vector outputs are written under `outputs/streamlit_sessions/session_<id>/vector_<id>/<timestamp>/`. Build the base with `session_manager.prepare_vector_output_base`; discover crawl inputs with `artifact_store.crawl_results.list_crawl_result_files`.
- Keep `session_id` and `crawl_id` separate. Browser sessions write under `outputs/streamlit_sessions/session_<id>/crawl_<id>/`.
- All file access for generated outputs must stay inside the session root. Use the existing path validation helpers instead of manual string checks.
- Background crawls should use `start_crawl_job()` and communicate through queue events. Do not block Streamlit reruns while a crawl is active.
- Cancellation must stay cooperative through `SiteCrawler.should_cancel`; do not terminate threads forcibly.
- Progress UI should consume crawler event mappings and helper estimates. Keep event keys stable unless tests and Streamlit consumers are updated together.
- Downloads should come from the generated-file listing helper and respect the app download-size guard. Folder rows in the download tree pick a Material icon by artifact type via `generated_files.download_folder_icon` (`crawl_*` → `:material/travel_explore:`, `vector_*` → `:material/database:`, otherwise `:material/folder_open:`).
- **Folder zip download.** Each top-level `crawl_*`/`vector_*` run folder gets a download-zip button (`:material/folder_zip:`) to the left of its **Delete this folder** button. The pure `generated_files.build_folder_zip_bytes(session_root, relative_path)` archives the folder's full contents (nested under the folder name, validated with `ensure_within_root`, refusing the session root) as `<folder>.zip`; `folder_zip_cache_token` keys an `@st.cache_data` wrapper so it rebuilds only when contents change. The button reuses the app download-size guard (`UI_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT_MB`) — oversized folders show a disabled "too large" button. Both helpers are unit-tested in `test_generated_files.py`.
- **Folder delete + shared confirm dialog.** Each top-level `crawl_*`/`vector_*` run folder in the download tree gets a red **Delete this folder** button (gated on `generated_files.is_run_folder`) that opens an `@st.dialog` confirmation driven by `delete_folder_relative_path` and calls the pure `generated_files.delete_generated_folder(session_root, relative_path)` — it validates with `ensure_within_root`, refuses the session root, `shutil.rmtree`s the folder, and prunes emptied parents. All confirm dialogs (crawl stop, vector stop, folder delete) share `dialog_ui.render_confirm_dialog` (green keep + red right-docked confirm) so look/UX stays consistent; only the `@st.dialog` wrapper, dismiss handler, and callbacks differ. The pure `confirm_dialog_css`, `is_run_folder`, and `delete_generated_folder` helpers are unit-tested.
- **Session retention** is configurable via `settings.session_retention_days` (`SESSION_RETENTION_DAYS`, default 7). `_run_startup_cleanup` passes it into `cleanup_old_sessions_with_lock`; do not hardcode the window. `cleanup_old_sessions(retention_days=...)` keeps `_DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS` only as a fallback default.
- The dev container starts Streamlit on attach at `0.0.0.0:8501`. If the port or startup command changes, update `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`, `README.md`, and `devcontainer.instructions.md` together.
- Tests must use `tmp_path` and mocked/stubbed crawl jobs. Never make real network requests from Streamlit tests.
- Streamlit tests follow the Streamlit Tests policy in [tests.instructions.md](./tests.instructions.md).
- **Translation catalog:** Whenever new user-facing text is added to the Streamlit UI, add both an English and an Indonesian entry to the `apps/streamlit/src/crawl4md_streamlit/i18n/` package first — `en.py` and `id.py` — then reference the key via `get_strings()` in the UI code. Never hardcode text directly in Streamlit components. **Library messages** (crawl/index warnings and errors) arrive as structured `LibraryMessage` data with a stable `code`; localize them with `i18n.localize_message` against `MESSAGE_CODES` (EN+ID), falling back to the library's English `default_text` — never substring-match library error text in the app. To add a new language, create a new `<code>.py` file in the package and register it in `__init__.py`.
- **Load Session dialog:** The 📁 button in `streamlit_app.py` (`_load_session_dialog`) lets users paste a session ID to restore access from another browser or device. Session IDs are validated with `validate_safe_id()` before any server-side path is constructed. `touch_session()` must be called after a successful load to reset the retention clock (`SESSION_RETENTION_DAYS`, default 7). The dialog must stay disabled while a crawl is running.
